codeReview.summary: Projection service implementation and route integration are mostly present, but the board-state list contract is not met: the API still exposes raw `status` instead of projected status.

issues.description: VAL-PROJ-030 requires the issue list API status to be projection-derived, but this route returns rows where projection is surfaced as `projectedStatus` while `status` remains the raw DB value.

`projectIssuesList()` returns `{ ...issue, ...proj }`, but `IssueProjection` has no `status` field, so `status` is never overwritten by projected state (e.g., active run+checkout can still return `status: "todo"`).
New tests are service-level only; there is no route-level assertion for VAL-PROJ-030/031 response semantics, which allowed this contract mismatch to slip through.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill guidance does not explicitly require HTTP route contract tests when API response semantics change, so feature work can pass with only service-level coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md (testing procedure) vs server/src/__tests__/projections.test.ts (service-only tests) and validation-contract.md:350-358 (API-level assertions).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared mission artifacts do not clearly disambiguate whether projections should replace `status` or be emitted as a separate field, which can lead to implementation drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:350-353 expects returned `status` to reflect projection, while server/src/routes/issues.ts:324-326 returns raw `status` plus `projectedStatus`.

summary: Reviewed feature projection-service (commit 7993d72b) using handoff, transcript skeleton, and diff. The feature adds projection service logic and wiring, but fails VAL-PROJ-030 contract alignment because list responses keep raw `status` instead of projected status.
